qa_pairs.question: How do you connect Visual Studio Code to an interactive compute node using the Remote SSH extension?
qa_pairs.answer: To connect Visual Studio Code to an interactive compute node, first update your `~/.ssh/config` file with the appropriate `ProxyJump` settings for your system (Narval or Rorqual). Next, in an external terminal connected via SSH, start a new interactive job using `salloc` with at least 2000M of memory, and make sure to note the allocated compute node name. Finally, in VS Code, open the Command Palette (Ctrl+Shift+P or Cmd+Shift+P), select 'Remote-SSH: Connect to Host...', and enter the noted compute node name, choosing 'Linux' if prompted for the operating system type.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum memory requirement for an interactive job when connecting Visual Studio Code to a compute node?
qa_pairs.answer: When connecting Visual Studio Code to a compute node, you must start an interactive job using `salloc` with at least 2000M of memory.

qa_pairs.question: On which specific login nodes is Visual Studio Code prohibited from being used?
qa_pairs.answer: Visual Studio Code is banned on the `tamIA` and `Fir` login nodes.
